title: Scaling Auditory Cognition via Test-Time Compute in Audio Language Models
abstract: The paper explores test-time compute (TTC) approaches to enhance the cognitive auditory capabilities of audio large language models (Audio LLMs), addressing their adaptation to real-world auditory environments with background noise, reverberation, and overlapping speech.
main_contribution: The paper proposes using test-time compute (TTC) to improve the cognitive auditory capabilities of audio LLMs without requiring additional fine-tuning.
methodology: The paper explores test-time compute (TTC) as a dynamic inference strategy that enables models to 'think longer' on more difficult problems, thereby improving inference performance.
limitations: Existing studies on audio LLMs were conducted on curated datasets, which limits their applicability to real-world auditory environments with background noise, reverberation, and overlapping speech.
future_work: The paper suggests exploring how audio LLMs can develop human-like cognitive strategies to maintain comprehension in adverse auditory environments.
